option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Email Implementation Summary ## ✅ What Was Implemented ### 1. **EmailTemplateUtil** - Template Management - **Location:** `/src/main/java/app/utility/email/EmailTemplateUtil.java` - **Features:** - Load templates by name (`welcome-email.html`, `mentor-email.html`, `mentee-email.html`) - Populate templates with dynamic values using `${key}` placeholders - Simple, reusable template system ### 2. **EmailBean** - Email Sending - **Location:** `/src/main/java/app/bean/EmailBean.java` - **Updated with:** - HTML email support (auto-detects HTML vs plain text) - Better error handling (authentication, messaging, unexpected errors) - SMTP configuration for Gmail - Clear console logging ### 3. **EmailObserverBean** - Event Listener - **Location:** `/src/main/java/app/bean/EmailObserverBean.java` - **Listens to:** `UserRegisteredEvent` - **Process:** 1. Catches registration events 2. Loads role-specific template 3. Populates template with user data 4. Sends email asynchronously (non-blocking) ### 4. **UserRegisteredEvent** - Enhanced Event - **Location:** `/src/main/java/app/bean/event/UserRegisteredEvent.java` - **Updated with:** - Added `role` field (USER, MENTOR, MENTEE) - Added `specialization` field (for mentors) - Multiple constructors for flexibility ### 5. **Email Templates** - Professional HTML - **In:** `EmailTemplateUtil.java` - **Three templates:** - Generic welcome (blue theme) - Mentor welcome (blue theme) - includes specialization - Mentee welcome (orange theme) - **Features:** - Responsive HTML - Professional styling - Dynamic placeholders ### 6. **Bean Integration** - Event Firing #### UserBean.registerUser() ``` User Registration → Fire UserRegisteredEvent ``` #### MentorBean.registerMentor() ``` Mentor Registration → Fire UserRegisteredEvent (with MENTOR role + specialization) ``` #### MenteeBean.registerMentee() ``` Mentee Registration → Fire UserRegisteredEvent (with MENTEE role) ``` --- ## Data Flow ``` User Registers ↓ Bean.registerUser/Mentor/Mentee() ↓ 1. Validate data 2. Save to database 3. Fire CRUD event (for audit) 4. Fire UserRegisteredEvent ↓ EmailObserverBean catches event (@Observes) ↓ @Asynchronous method executes in background thread ↓ EmailTemplateUtil.loadTemplate() → loads role-specific template EmailTemplateUtil.populateTemplate() → fills ${placeholders} EmailBean.sendEmail() → sends via SMTP ↓ User receives email (non-blocking!) ``` --- ## Files Created/Modified ### Created: ✅ `/src/main/java/app/utility/email/EmailTemplateUtil.java` ✅ `/EMAIL_SETUP_GUIDE.md` ### Modified: ✅ `/src/main/java/app/bean/EmailBean.java` - Added HTML support ✅ `/src/main/java/app/bean/EmailObserverBean.java` - Template integration ✅ `/src/main/java/app/bean/event/UserRegisteredEvent.java` - Enhanced with role/specialization ✅ `/src/main/java/app/bean/UserBean.java` - Added event firing ✅ `/src/main/java/app/bean/MentorBean.java` - Added mentor email event ✅ `/src/main/java/app/bean/MenteeBean.java` - Added mentee email event --- ## Key Features ### 1. Non-Blocking Execution - Uses `@Asynchronous` decorator - Registration completes immediately - Email sent in background thread - Better user experience ### 2. Event-Driven Architecture - Observer pattern (EmailObserverBean observes UserRegisteredEvent) - Decoupled: beans don't directly call email service - Easy to add more observers (SMS, notifications, etc.) ### 3. Role-Based Content - Different templates for different roles - Mentors get specialization in their email - Each role gets customized welcome message ### 4. Template System - Simple placeholder replacement - Easy to customize without changing code - Supports HTML emails with professional styling ### 5. Error Handling - Catches authentication errors - Catches messaging errors - Catches unexpected errors - Logs meaningful messages for debugging --- ## Security Considerations ### Gmail App Password - ✓ More secure than account password - ✓ Can be revoked independently - ✓ Limited scope (email only) - ✓ Requires 2FA enabled ### Email Storage - ✓ Credentials in Java code (for now) - Future: Move to configuration file / environment variables - Future: Use keystore for production --- ## Usage ### Quick Start: 1. **Update credentials** in `EmailBean.java`: ```java private final String username = "your-email@gmail.com"; private final String password = "your-app-password"; ``` 2. **Register a user** - Email automatically sends! 3. **Check logs**: ``` [EmailObserverBean] User registered event received for: john [EmailBean] Attempting to send email to: john@example.com [EmailBean] ✓ Email sent successfully to john@example.com ``` --- ## Customization Examples ### Add a new field to email: 1. Add to `UserRegisteredEvent`: ```java private final String organization; ``` 2. Update constructor: ```java public UserRegisteredEvent(String email, String name, String role, String organization) { this.organization = organization; } ``` 3. Update template: ```java "organization", event.getOrganization() ``` 4. Use in template: ```html

Organization: ${organization}

``` --- ## Testing Checklist - [ ] Update EmailBean credentials - [ ] Register a new USER - Check email received ✓ - Check content matches template ✓ - [ ] Register a new MENTOR - Check email includes specialization ✓ - [ ] Register a new MENTEE - Check MENTEE-specific email received ✓ - [ ] Check console logs for errors - [ ] Check email also goes to audit trail --- ## Related Classes - `UserBean` - Fires events on user registration - `MentorBean` - Fires events on mentor registration - `MenteeBean` - Fires events on mentee registration - `AuditTrailBean` - Observes CRUD events (separate from email) - `EmailObserverBean` - Observes registration events --- ## Documentation See: `/EMAIL_SETUP_GUIDE.md` for detailed setup and troubleshooting

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Email System - Quick Reference
## ⚡ 3-Step Setup
### Step 1: Get Gmail App Password - Go to: https://myaccount.google.com/apppasswords - Generate password for "Mail" - Copy 16-character password
### Step 2: Update Credentials File: `EmailBean.java` (line 12-13) ```java private final String username = "your-email@gmail.com"; private final String password = "abcdefghijklmnop"; // 16 chars, no spaces ```
### Step 3: Test - Register a new user - Check email received - Done! ✓
---
## How It Works (Simple)
``` Register User ↓ Bean fires UserRegisteredEvent ↓ EmailObserverBean catches event ↓ Loads template (mentor/mentee/generic) ↓ Populates ${name}, ${role}, etc ↓ Sends email ↓ User gets email (doesn't block registration) ```
---
## What Gets Sent
### USER Registration - Template: `welcome-email.html` - Content: Generic welcome
### MENTOR Registration - Template: `mentor-email.html` - Content: Mentor welcome + specialization
### MENTEE Registration - Template: `mentee-email.html` - Content: Mentee welcome + next steps
---
## Files to Know
| File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `EmailBean.java` | Sends emails (UPDATE CREDENTIALS HERE) | | `EmailObserverBean.java` | Listens to registration events | | `EmailTemplateUtil.java` | Loads/populates templates | | `UserRegisteredEvent.java` | Event fired on registration |
---
## Customizing Templates
File: `EmailTemplateUtil.java`
Methods: - `getWelcomeTemplate()` - Edit for generic users - `getMentorTemplate()` - Edit for mentors - `getMenteeTemplate()` - Edit for mentees
Placeholders available: - `${name}` - Username - `${role}` - USER/MENTOR/MENTEE - `${specialization}` - Mentor specialization
---
## If Email Doesn't Send
1. **Check credentials** in `EmailBean.java` - Is email correct? - Is app password correct (16 chars)? - No spaces in password?
2. **Check Gmail settings** - Is 2FA enabled? - Is app password generated?
3. **Check console logs** - Error message? - Network issue? - Gmail blocking?
4. **Check spam folder** - Is email there? - Add to contacts if needed
---
## What Happens When User Registers
1. **UserBean/MentorBean/MenteeBean** validates and saves user 2. **Fires UserRegisteredEvent** with email, name, role, (specialization for mentors) 3. **EmailObserverBean** catches event asynchronously 4. **Loads template** based on role 5. **Replaces** `${placeholders}` with actual values 6. **Sends email** via Gmail SMTP 7. **Logs result** to console
All happens in **background thread** - registration completes immediately!
---
## Pro Tips
- ✓ **App Password only** - Don't use regular Gmail password - ✓ **2FA must be enabled** - Requirement for app passwords - ✓ **No spaces in password** - Copy carefully - ✓ **Check spam folder** - First test might go to spam - ✓ **Test with real email** - Use your own email first
---
## Integration in Your Code
### When someone registers (e.g., in registerUser):
```java // Save user... userDAO.addUser(user);
// Fire event! userRegisteredEvent.fire(new UserRegisteredEvent( user.getEmail(), user.getUsername(), user.getRole() )); ```
That's it! Email system handles the rest.
---
## Event Flow Diagram
``` UserBean ↓ registerUser() ↓ userDAO.addUser(user) ✓ ↓ userRegisteredEvent.fire( new UserRegisteredEvent(email, name, role) ) ↓ @Asynchronous ← Email sent in background! ↓ EmailObserverBean.onUserRegistered() ↓ EmailTemplateUtil.loadTemplate() ↓ EmailTemplateUtil.populateTemplate() ↓ EmailBean.sendEmail() ↓ ✓ Email sent (doesn't block user registration) ```
---
## Support
For detailed setup: See `EMAIL_SETUP_GUIDE.md` For implementation details: See `EMAIL_IMPLEMENTATION.md`

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Email Template System Setup Guide
## Overview Your email system is now set up with: - **EmailTemplateUtil** - Loads and populates email templates - **EmailBean** - Sends HTML emails via SMTP - **EmailObserverBean** - Listens to registration events and sends emails - **UserRegisteredEvent** - Event fired when users register - Role-based templates for **USER**, **MENTOR**, and **MENTEE**
---
## ⚙️ Configuration
### Step 1: Get Gmail App Password (for Gmail users)
1. Go to: https://myaccount.google.com/apppasswords 2. Select "Mail" and "Windows Computer" (or your device) 3. Click "Generate" 4. Copy the **16-character password** (remove spaces)
### Step 2: Update EmailBean Credentials
Edit: `/src/main/java/app/bean/EmailBean.java`
```java private final String username = "your-email@gmail.com"; // CHANGE THIS private final String password = "your-app-password"; // CHANGE THIS (paste 16-char app password) ```
**Example:** ```java private final String username = "mentorke.app@gmail.com"; private final String password = "abcd efgh ijkl mnop"; // Remove spaces: abcdefghijklmnop ```
---
## How It Works
### Email Flow
1. **User registers** (mentee/mentor/user) 2. **Bean fires UserRegisteredEvent** with: - Email address - Username - Role (USER/MENTOR/MENTEE) - Specialization (for mentors only)
3. **EmailObserverBean catches event** - Loads appropriate template based on role - Replaces `${name}`, `${role}`, `${specialization}` with actual values - Calls EmailBean.sendEmail()
4. **Email is sent asynchronously** using `@Asynchronous` - Doesn't block the registration process - Happens in background
---
## Email Templates
### Available Templates
1. **welcome-email.html** - Generic welcome for users 2. **mentor-email.html** - Personalized for mentors 3. **mentee-email.html** - Personalized for mentees
### Template Placeholders
Templates use `${key}` placeholders: - `${name}` - Username - `${role}` - User role (MENTOR/MENTEE/USER) - `${specialization}` - Specialization (mentors only)
### Customizing Templates
To customize emails, edit `EmailTemplateUtil.java`:
**Location:** `/src/main/java/app/utility/email/EmailTemplateUtil.java`
**Methods to edit:** - `getWelcomeTemplate()` - Generic user template - `getMentorTemplate()` - Mentor-specific template - `getMenteeTemplate()` - Mentee-specific template
**Example - Add new placeholder:** ```java String html = EmailTemplateUtil.populateTemplate(template, Map.of( "name", event.getName(), "role", event.getRole(), "specialization", event.getSpecialization() != null ? event.getSpecialization() : "", "newField", "new value" // Add this )); ```
---
## Integration Points
### In UserBean (Generic users) ```java userRegisteredEvent.fire(new UserRegisteredEvent( user.getEmail(), user.getUsername(), user.getRole() )); ```
### In MentorBean (Mentors) ```java userRegisteredEvent.fire(new UserRegisteredEvent( user.getEmail(), user.getUsername(), "MENTOR", mentor.getSpecialization() // Mentor-specific data )); ```
### In MenteeBean (Mentees) ```java userRegisteredEvent.fire(new UserRegisteredEvent( user.getEmail(), user.getUsername(), "MENTEE" )); ```
---
## Troubleshooting
### Email Not Sending?
Check console logs for: 1. **Authentication failed** - Wrong email/password 2. **Messaging error** - SMTP connection issue 3. **Unexpected error** - Network/firewall issue
### For Gmail: - ✓ Use **App Password** (not your regular password) - ✓ Enable 2FA on your Gmail account - ✓ Copy password WITHOUT spaces - ✓ Use correct sender email in both places
### Testing Email
Register a new user and check: 1. Console logs for `[EmailBean]` messages 2. Recipient's email inbox (and spam folder) 3. EmailObserverBean logs
---
## Code Examples
### Add a new email trigger
1. **Create a new Event** (if needed): ```java public class EmailEvent { private String email; private String subject; // ... etc } ```
2. **Create an observer**: ```java @Asynchronous public void onEmailEvent(@Observes EmailEvent event) { String html = EmailTemplateUtil.loadTemplate("template.html"); emailBean.sendEmail(event.getEmail(), event.getSubject(), html); } ```
3. **Fire the event**: ```java emailEvent.fire(new EmailEvent(...)); ```
---
## ✅ Summary
Your email system is **ready to use**: - ✓ Templates configured - ✓ Async sending with `@Asynchronous` - ✓ Role-based email content - ✓ Event-driven architecture (Observer pattern) - ✓ Simple to extend
**Just update your Gmail credentials and you're done!**

option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
